description: These are the current main headlines from sources across US. Headlines refresh every 900 seconds, so they may lag by a few minutes. For context, this response also includes the two latest AI-generated overviews of the headline landscape, plus yesterday's micro-historical overview.

headlines.headline: As Ebola Spreads in East Africa, Will China Step Up?
headlines.subtitle: China is well positioned to help stop the deadly virus, and could fill the void left by the U.S.

headlines.headline: US intercepts six Iranian missiles after American military strikes Iran twice
Three fathers killed their families this week as domestic violence deaths remain high
headlines.subtitle: Fathers slaughtered their families in three states this week. For those who track this kind of violence, the killings are both predictable and preventable.

headlines.headline: US shoots down drones threatening Strait of Hormuz traffic
Female Navy officers say they fear a career cap after Hegseth cuts women from promotions list
headlines.subtitle: Several female Navy officers say they see Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s recent intervention in a promotions list as a sign their careers now have a ceiling.

headlines.headline: Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire as stalemate continues in peace talks
headlines.subtitle: The U.S. military reported that it shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz overnight Saturday.

headlines.headline: Israel has reportedly used white phosphorus near Lebanese cities and towns. What is it?
headlines.subtitle: White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.

headlines.headline: She helped America win WWII. Meet a real-life 'Rosie the Riveter'
headlines.subtitle: The "Rosie the Riveter" campaign recruited women to work jobs supporting US troops during WWII. Jennifer McMullen, now 101 years old, was one of them.

headlines.headline: Democrats can do better than Graham Platner. They must demand he drop out.
Police hunt suspect after 12 people shot at family-friendly summer festival
headlines.subtitle: Multiple people were rushed to the hospital after shots were fired at what was supposed to be a family-friendly festival in Ohio on Saturday.

headlines.headline: Cut Platner Loose
headlines.subtitle: Just a few years ago, Republicans sacrificed an Alabama Senate seat rather than endure an unacceptable colleague. Will Democrats do the same in Maine?

headlines.headline: How Virginia Democrats are coping with their redistricting defeat
headlines.subtitle: Three takeaways from a key swing district in Virginia.

headlines.headline: Native Americans Taught Colonists How To Fight—and To Live Without Kings
headlines.subtitle: Native Americans Taught Colonists How To Fight—and To Live Without Kings

headlines.headline: California CheatingDemocrats Are Rigging the Election in CA…But Are They Doing It Legally?
headlines.subtitle: Friday on “The Alex Marlow Show,” Breitbart Editor-in-Chief Alex Marlow discussed the ongoing counting process in California’s elections.

headlines.headline: The Empire Still Wants to Destroy the Cuban Revolution
headlines.subtitle: Trump’s latest moves against Cuba show that Washington has never accepted the island’s defiance of US power. Now is the time for solidarity with the Cuban people.

headlines.headline: New Questions Emerge About Platner’s Biography As Scrutiny Intensifies
“Warehousing Human Beings”
headlines.subtitle: Former immigration judge Andrea Sáenz and American Immigration Council’s Aaron Reichlin-Melnick on the conditions at Delaney Hall and other ICE detention centers across the U.S.

headlines.headline: HEGSETH PROMOTION MEDDLING ALARM
headlines.subtitle: 'NOT THE NORM'

headlines.headline: Trump Warned to Stay Away From Knicks Game
headlines.subtitle: The president’s attendance will trigger a massive security operation.

headlines.headline: Trump Is Remaking Latin America in His Image; Peru and Colombia May Be Next
headlines.subtitle: Across Latin America, a wave of right-wing governments has taken power over the past two years, reshaping the region’s map.

headlines.headline: Source: US to Use Iranian Assets to Aid Gulf Allies
headlines.subtitle:
The United States will make Iranian assets available to Gulf allies to ​support rebuilding and repairs for future ⁠damage caused ​by Iran, a source said on Saturday. The U.S. may also use the assets to support repairs for past damages.
            [Full Story]

headlines.headline: Who Really Won The Midterms' Gerrymandering War?
Restraining Israel Is Not the Answer
Teen thug points gun in face of Marine Corps veteran, demands his car keys. But punk definitely picked wrong victim.
Are Democrats Blowing a Golden Opportunity to Beat Susan Collins?
headlines.subtitle: Jim Newell

current.headline: Ceasefire Strained by New Strikes
current.summary: The media focuses on the Iran conflict, with new US and Iran strikes straining the ceasefire (CNN, CBS). Hegseth's D-Day speech linking immigration to 'invasion' draws criticism (AP, Daily Beast). The Platner scandal continues with calls for him to drop out (Atlantic, MSNBC, Slate, Federalist).

previous.headline: Iran's No-Choice Deal
previous.summary: The media focuses on the escalating Iran conflict, with Trump stating Iran 'has no choice' but to agree to a deal (Newsmax, NBC News). Meanwhile, Hegseth's controversial D-Day speech and cold welcome in France draw attention (Daily Beast, Washington Examiner). The Platner scandal continues with new questions (Daily Wire, MSNBC, Slate, Federalist).
